title: gh-actions-pin run provenance
description: Structured, action-centric provenance document written at the end of a `gh actions-pin` run. Records what was resolved and how, deduplicating actions (one entry per unique action@ref) and listing the workflows that reference each one.

schema.description: Provenance schema version. Major-only versioning (no minor/patch): a v1 reader will refuse v2 documents outright.

generated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp marking when the report was written.

actions.description: Deduplicated list of action dependencies, one entry per unique action@ref.

auto_fixed.description: Auto-fix rewrites the run applied (impostor pins replaced with a sane release). One entry per (workflow, action) pair. Omitted when no auto-fixes happened.

tool.description: Identifies the tool and version that produced the report.

repo.description: Identifies the repository the run scanned.

summary.description: Run roll-up: counts by resolution plus overall validity.

action.description: A single deduplicated action dependency and the provenance of how it was resolved.

nwo.description: Owner/name of the action's repository (e.g. actions/checkout).

ref.description: The ref the workflow asked for (tag, branch, or SHA).

sha.description: The pinned commit, in algo-prefixed digest form (e.g. sha1-...). Omitted when the run did not produce a pin.

hash_algo.description: Hash algorithm of `sha` (e.g. sha1, sha256). Omitted when `sha` is omitted.

observed_sha.description: The SHA the resolver got when it looked up `ref` during this run. Recorded when it differs from `sha` (the pinned value) — e.g. MISLEADING_SHA, REF_MOVED, LOCKFILE_FORGERY. Empty when the run did not surface a divergence.

direct.description: True when the action is referenced directly by a workflow; false when it is a transitive dependency of a composite action.

resolution.description: What was done to this action during the run.

how.description: Concise, human-readable provenance (e.g. "locked ref v4 to <sha>", "verified via full branch scan", a security reason).

issue.description: Originating finding category (e.g. MISSING, REF_MOVED) when the action needed work; empty when it was already valid. Loose string — the finding taxonomy is owned by the CLI and may evolve independently of this schema.

canonical_branch.description: Whether the commit was found on a canonical branch. Omitted when reachability was not decided (e.g. unresolved actions).

workflows.description: Every workflow file that references this action, so the action is recorded once rather than repeated per workflow.

required_by.description: Parent composite actions for transitive dependencies.

auto_fix.description: A single auto-fix rewrite applied during remediation. Records what the workflow used to reference and what the run rewrote it to.

workflow.description: Workflow file path the rewrite was applied to.

from_ref.description: The ref the workflow originally used (before the rewrite).

from_sha.description: The commit SHA the original ref resolved to, when known. Empty when the original ref was a tag and the SHA wasn't recorded.

to_ref.description: The ref the workflow now uses (after the rewrite).

to_sha.description: The commit SHA the new ref resolves to. Empty when unknown.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the rewrite was performed.
